# `api/types.ts`

This module defines the core TypeScript types and interfaces used throughout the API layer, including todo item shapes, request bodies, and standardized response envelopes.

---

## Interfaces

### `Todo`

Represents a single todo item.

| Member | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `string` | Unique identifier |
| `title` | `string` | Display text of the todo |
| `completed` | `boolean` | Whether the todo has been completed |
| `createdAt` | `string` | ISO timestamp when the todo was created |

**Example:**

```ts
const todo: Todo = {
  id: "abc-123",
  title: "Buy groceries",
  completed: false,
  createdAt: "2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z",
};
```

---

### `CreateTodoRequest`

Request body for creating a new todo.

| Member | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `title` | `string` | Display text of the todo to create |

**Example:**

```ts
const request: CreateTodoRequest = {
  title: "Buy groceries",
};
```

---

### `UpdateTodoRequest`

Request body for updating an existing todo. All fields are optional — include only the fields you want to change.

| Member | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `title` | `string` *(optional)* | Updated display text |
| `completed` | `boolean` *(optional)* | Updated completion status |

**Example:**

```ts
const request: UpdateTodoRequest = {
  completed: true,
};
```

---

### `ApiResponse<T>`

Standard API response envelope returned on successful requests.

| Member | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `data` | `T` | The response payload |
| `ok` | `true` | Discriminant literal indicating success |

**Example:**

```ts
const response: ApiResponse<Todo> = {
  ok: true,
  data: {
    id: "abc-123",
    title: "Buy groceries",
    completed: false,
    createdAt: "2024-01-15T10:30:00.000Z",
  },
};
```

---

### `ApiError`

Standard API error envelope returned when a request fails.

| Member | Type | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `error` | `string` | Human-readable error message |
| `ok` | `false` | Discriminant literal indicating failure |

**Example:**

```ts
const errorResponse: ApiError = {
  ok: false,
  error: "Todo not found",
};
```

---

## Usage Notes

`ApiResponse<T>` and `ApiError` share the discriminant property `ok` and are intended to be used together as a union type, allowing exhaustive narrowing at call sites:

```ts
function handleResponse(result: ApiResponse<Todo> | ApiError) {
  if (result.ok) {
    console.log(result.data); // ApiResponse<Todo>
  } else {
    console.error(result.error); // ApiError
  }
}
```
